author: InfluencerNGZK
text:
Hey I am using the following code to get cookies

```
from tbselenium.tbdriver import TorBrowserDriver, webdriver
import time

with TorBrowserDriver("/home/rena/Documents/tor-browser-linux64-9.0.9_zh-CN/tor-browser_zh-CN") as driver:
    driver.get('https://github.com')
    print(driver.get_cookies())
    time.sleep(3)
```

It does not print any cookies. However when I try firefox, I can get the cookies

```
from tbselenium.tbdriver import TorBrowserDriver, webdriver

driver = webdriver.Firefox()
driver.set_window_size(800, 600)
driver.get("https://github.com")
print(driver.get_cookies())
```

The cookies get from firefox are listed below.  
`[{'name': '_gh_sess', 'value': 'j2xFUjFzKIyGKK2Dal3fCjuPk0mVC%2Fg06ekgN7GaaCSAUdZPVo0aVzhk3OEaDqbUeBBTT2G7qB%2FLQHdjB3bUi4%2BCbnxfB0KoxLlppYHcw6u1ALovXJDi4pcCsBc%2Bxl0H1bjgwtxbAftvo4o45yjZCmMh33odEYyjOgFiCaYAx%2Ft8DrMN2EkuGM8bvn8hZx6sOEvl%2Fz%2FRsPtDZmz52taUqlYzK6dnmk1TWe8Ti97pTOKfVlIp6%2B7Wa%2BlugYM53CJ9aUNqZps6QBiMZm4rRT1V%2Fg%3D%3D--hvB%2FGvQS0roaCwgr--CMVnjkIi9iRoV4CSbUysbA%3D%3D', 'path': '/', 'domain': 'github.com', 'secure': True, 'httpOnly': True}, {'name': '_octo', 'value': 'GH1.1.1571672737.1594648589', 'path': '/', 'domain': '.github.com', 'secure': True, 'httpOnly': False, 'expiry': 1626184589}, {'name': 'logged_in', 'value': 'no', 'path': '/', 'domain': '.github.com', 'secure': True, 'httpOnly': True, 'expiry': 1626184589}, {'name': '_ga', 'value': 'GA1.2.988609455.1594648592', 'path': '/', 'domain': '.github.com', 'secure': False, 'httpOnly': False, 'expiry': 1657720591}, {'name': '_gat', 'value': '1', 'path': '/', 'domain': '.github.com', 'secure': False, 'httpOnly': False, 'expiry': 1594649191}]`  
What is the difference between the two methods above. How can I get cookies from torbrowser driver just as firefox driver do.

author: InfluencerNGZK
text:
Seems that the following code can solve the cookies problem mentioned above

```
from selenium import webdriver
import time
import os
from selenium.webdriver.firefox.firefox_profile import FirefoxProfile
from selenium.webdriver.firefox.firefox_binary import FirefoxBinary
from selenium.webdriver.firefox.options import Options

profileTor = '/etc/tor/'  # torrc
binary = os.path.expanduser('/home/rena/Documents/tor-browser-linux64-9.0.9_zh-CN/tor-browser_zh-CN/Browser/firefox')

firefox_binary = FirefoxBinary(binary)
firefox_profile = FirefoxProfile(profileTor)

# set socks proxy
firefox_profile.set_preference("network.proxy.type", 1)
firefox_profile.set_preference("network.proxy.socks_version", 5)
firefox_profile.set_preference("network.proxy.socks", '127.0.0.1')
firefox_profile.set_preference("network.proxy.socks_port", 9150)
firefox_profile.set_preference("network.proxy.socks_remote_dns", True)

options = Options()
options.set_headless(headless=False)

driver = webdriver.Firefox(firefox_profile=firefox_profile, firefox_options=options)
driver.get('http://check.torproject.org')
time.sleep(3)
print(driver.get_cookies())
driver.close()
```
